original_abstract: Despite contributing the least to global greenhouse gas emissions, Africa remains disproportionately vulnerable to the escalating impacts of human-induced climate change. Across the continent, critical development sectors are already grappling with extensive losses and damages (high confidence). These climate-driven disruptions manifest as significant biodiversity loss, acute water shortages, and diminished agricultural productivity, which collectively undermine economic stability and lead to tragic loss of life.
humanized_abstract: Africa, despite being the continent that emits the least amount of Greenhouse gases (GHGs) that cause climate change, is currently suffering the worst impacts of climate change. It is agreed upon with high degree of confidence that most development areas in Africa are already experiencing impacts amounting to loss and damage. The current impacts of climate change being experienced in Africa are affecting greatly biodiversity, water resources and agriculture, which are the backbone of many economies in the continent and also results in loss of lives and dignity.
